items.title: UK’s Financial Conduct Authority Leads London Crackdown on Crypto Traders
items.desc: Multi-agency raid marks shift from warnings to physical enforcement as UK regulator finds zero legally registered P2P crypto traders.

items.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Presses Senate to Pass Crypto Market Structure Legislation
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine U.S. Treasury Secretary Presses Senate to Pass Crypto Market Structure Legislation Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told a Senate panel Wednesday that passing comprehensive crypto legislation is vital to maintaining U.S. financial leadership. This post U.S. Treasury Secretary Presses Senate to Pass Crypto Market Structure Legislation first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: SparkLend Sees Over $1B in Deposits Since Kelp Exploit as Aave TVL Plunges
items.desc: Aave TVL has dropped by $10B since the Kelp attackers used the protocol to borrow $190 million in WETH, depositing unbacked rsETH.

items.title: Thailand regulator mulls crypto futures expansion in licensing overhaul
items.desc: Thailand’s SEC is seeking feedback on rule changes that would let digital asset firms offer derivatives within existing entities, lowering barriers to market entry.

items.title: Kraken calls for de minimus exemption on crypto taxes after 2025 reports
items.desc: The crypto exchange advocated for two key changes to US tax law affecting crypto users to “eliminate millions of unnecessary forms.”

items.title: TD Cowen flags five hurdles to crypto bill beyond stablecoin yield issue
items.desc: These include a lack of CFTC commissioners, conflicts tied to World Liberty Financial, and concerns around Iran's use of crypto payments.

items.title: Robinhood Venture Fund's $75M stake in OpenAI aims to enable retail investments
items.desc: The private equity investment will be used to give retail customers indirect price exposure to OpenAI through venture tokens.

items.title: Base Pushes Toward Final L2 Stage with First Independent Upgrade
items.desc: Base's Azul upgrade is currently live on testnet, with mainnet launch scheduled for mid-May.

items.title: Crypto bill nears stablecoin rewards breakthrough as talks hit ‘good spot,’ but ethics concerns set to resurface
items.desc: After months of negotiations over how a crypto bill should treat stablecoin rewards, a key negotiator says discussions are in a "good spot."

items.title: 'Axie Infinity' Gaming Network Ronin Sets Date for Ethereum Layer-2 Migration
items.desc: The Ronin gaming network will slash RON token inflation from over 20% to less than 1% while introducing automated developer rewards.

items.title: Banks seek to slow down implementation of crypto's GENIUS Act on stablecoin oversight
items.desc: U.S. banking groups argued that a number of federal agencies are moving quickly on stablecoin regulations, making it hard to understand how rules will interact.

items.title: The Protocol: Kelp DAO exploited for $292 million
items.desc: Also: DPRK hacking crypto, Aave contagion and Coinbase on quantum computing.

items.title: Ethereum risks 10% decline versus Bitcoin despite record ETH staking
items.desc: Ethereum’s record 32.33% staking ratio is shrinking liquid supply, reducing sell pressure and potentially supporting an ETH price recovery over time.

items.title: Russia’s Sberbank Ready to Enter Crypto Trading as Russia Moves Toward Regulation
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Russia’s Sberbank Ready to Enter Crypto Trading as Russia Moves Toward Regulation Sberbank will provide access to crypto trading once regulation and organized exchange trading begin, Senior Vice President Ruslan Vesterovsky said. This post Russia’s Sberbank Ready to Enter Crypto Trading as Russia Moves Toward Regulation first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Crypto Long & Short: Protecting the people building DeFi infrastructure
items.desc: In this week’s Crypto Long & Short Newsletter, Jennifer Rosenthal writes on the need to protect the people actually building DeFi infrastructure. Then, Alexis Sirkia comments on how Ethereum's L2 strategy is failing due to a fundamental design flaw.

items.title: The $292 million Kelp DAO exploit shows why crypto bridges are still one of the industry's weakest links
items.desc: The problem is structural and as long as bridges depend on complex systems with shared infrastructure and hidden trust assumptions, they will remain vulnerable.

items.title: GSR Launches Actively Managed Bitcoin, Ethereum and Solana Basket ETF on Nasdaq
items.desc: Crypto market maker GSR has launched an actively managed ETF on the Nasdaq that includes Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana.

items.title: Input Output proposes scaled-back Cardano treasury plan ahead of Leios mainnet push
items.desc: The Leios mainnet launch is at the center of the roadmap, but IO’s tracker shows the upgrade still mid-development.

items.title: American Bitcoin ($ABTC) Activates 11,000 New Bitcoin Miners, Expanding Hashrate Capacity
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine American Bitcoin ($ABTC) Activates 11,000 New Bitcoin Miners, Expanding Hashrate Capacity American Bitcoin (ABTC) announced it had energized more than 11,000 new mining machines at its Drumheller facility. This post American Bitcoin ($ABTC) Activates 11,000 New Bitcoin Miners, Expanding Hashrate Capacity first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Bitcoin tops $79,000 as crypto rally gathers steam; Circle, Coinbase, Strategy lead
items.desc: BTC pushed to an 11-week high with dynamics for a short squeeze building, one analyst noted.

items.title: Russia Advances Sweeping Crypto Bill With Provisions for 'Circumventing Sanctions'
items.desc: The legislation would license crypto exchanges and permit cross-border cryptocurrency use while maintaining domestic payment restrictions.

items.title: The London P2P sweep: UK FCA raids eight illegal peer-to-peer trading hubs
items.desc: The sites were targeted for facilitating P2P trading without required registration or anti-money laundering controls, posing a financial crime risk.

items.title: Crypto giant GSR launches its first ETF to give investors an easy way to bet on the big 3 tokens
items.desc: GSR is entering the asset management space with a new Nasdaq-listed ETF that actively manages a basket of bitcoin, ether and solana while offering investors a chance to earn staking yields.

items.title: UK FCA carries out first crackdown on illegal peer-to-peer crypto trading
items.desc: The Financial Conduct Authority said it has carried out its first operation targeting illegal peer-to-peer crypto trading in the UK.

items.title: Binance.US slashes trading fees across listed cryptocurrencies in a bid to attract users
items.desc: “American crypto traders have been paying too much for too long,” Binance.US CEO Stephen Gregory said about the move to slash fees.

items.title: Lazarus-linked macOS malware hits crypto and fintech firms
items.desc: Security researchers linked a new “Mach-O Man” malware kit to a Lazarus campaign that uses fake meeting invites and ClickFix prompts to steal credentials and access corporate systems on macOS.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.desc: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Tax-free bitcoin is back: How UK investors can avoid paying duty on crypto investments once more
items.desc: Stratiphy will offer access to three ETNs provided by 21Shares: those covering bitcoin, ether and one combining BTC and gold.

items.title: Binance.US cuts spot trading fees to near zero in push to undercut rivals
items.desc: The exchange set maker fees at 0% and taker fees at 0.02% across all pairs, extending near-zero pricing to all users with no volume tiers or subscription requirements.

items.title: Russia Advances Sweeping Crypto Regulation Bill, Legalizes Cross-Border Payments and Tightens Market Controls
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Russia Advances Sweeping Crypto Regulation Bill, Legalizes Cross-Border Payments and Tightens Market Controls Russia’s State Duma advanced a sweeping bill to regulate digital currencies, marking a major step toward formal oversight of the country’s crypto market. This post Russia Advances Sweeping Crypto Regulation Bill, Legalizes Cross-Border Payments and Tightens Market Controls first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Aave deposits fall by $15B as Kelp exploit sparks flight from DeFi lender
items.desc: Aave’s supplied balance has tanked since the Kelp DAO bridge exploit, as users pull funds amid uncertainty over how much of the rsETH-linked shortfall the protocol will ultimately absorb.

items.title: Armed intruders force French family to transfer $820,000 in crypto: report
items.desc: Two armed men forced a French family to transfer $820,000 in crypto during a home invasion, local media reported.

items.title: UK cracks down on illegal peer-to-peer crypto trading in nationwide raids
items.desc: The Financial Conduct Authority has raided eight locations suspected of illegal peer-to-peer crypto trading, issuing cease-and-desist orders.

items.title: Europe’s MiCA regime puts smaller crypto firms under pressure
items.desc: Smaller crypto companies across Europe face mounting compliance costs as MiCA moves from framework to enforcement, raising fears of market consolidation.

items.title: CoinDesk 20 performance update: Aptos (APT) rises 5.5%, leading index higher
items.desc: Internet Computer (ICP) up 5.3% from Tuesday, joined Aptos (APT) as a top performer.

items.title: Russia advances crypto bill that could pave way for criminal penalties
items.desc: Russian lawmakers passed a first reading of a bill regulating crypto through licensed intermediaries, with key rules set to take effect in July 2026 and 2027.

items.title: 'Finding Satoshi' Makes the Case for Hal Finney, Len Sassaman as Bitcoin Co-Creators
items.desc: A new documentary asserts that Bitcoin creator Satoshi Nakamoto was actually two people: the late Hal Finney and Len Sassaman.

items.title: ‘I suspect you got to the right answer’: New Satoshi documentary makes the case Hal Finney and Len Sassaman were Bitcoin’s co-creators
items.desc: The film follows a four-year investigation led by New York Times bestselling author William D. Cohan and private investigator Tyler Maroney.

items.title: GSR enters crypto ETF field with actively managed multi-asset fund tracking Bitcoin, Ethereum and Solana
items.desc: Core3 marks GSR's first crypto ETF offering amid a period of expansion for the major market maker and the wider crypto fund sector.

items.desc: WalletConnect's ecosystem of 700 crypto wallets and 70,000 dApps now supports the enterprise- and privacy-focused blockchain network.

items.title: Kraken filed 56 million crypto tax forms for 2025. One-third were below $1
items.desc: The lack of a de minimis exemption for crypto payments and staking rewards taxed at receipt creates a huge reporting burden, the data shows.

items.desc: Infinite is introducing bank accounts that support traditional and crypto services like ACH, wire and stablecoin transfers.

items.title: North Korea tied to heists worth $578M in April after Kelp DAO exploit

items.title: SEC ‘on the cusp’ of onchain tokenized securities exemption: Atkins
items.desc: SEC Chair Paul Atkins says the SEC is “on the cusp” of an innovation exemption to enable compliant onchain trading of tokenized securities.

items.title: Lazarus Group has become especially dangerous with new Mach-O Man attack: CertiK
items.desc: North Korea's Lazarus Group has a new attack vector that allows it to exploit an apparently routine business call as a gateway into a target's systems.

items.title: Onchain vault provider Upshift taps Securitize Fund Services for third-party reporting

items.desc: Base rolled out its Azul upgrade on testnet, introducing multiproofs as the Coinbase-incubated Ethereum L2 eyes Stage 2 decentralization.

items.title: Coinbase shifts New York prediction markets fight to federal court

items.desc: BTC hovers at key $78,000 level with $180 million liquidations at stake, while altcoins and memecoins surge as risk-on sentiment returns.

items.desc: Stratiphy secured IFISA approval, reopening a tax-free ISA route for UK retail investors to hold 21Shares crypto ETNs.

items.desc: Volo Protocol has confirmed a $3.5 million exploit affecting select vaults, adding that it has frozen assets and started fund recovery efforts amid ongoing investigation.

items.desc: Funds leaving Aave are splitting across safer lending, simpler ETH exposure and off-chain yield, with stablecoins acting as a temporary refuge.

items.desc: Stratiphy is reopening a tax-free route to crypto ETNs in the UK after HMRC’s ISA rule change left retail investors with little practical access.

items.title: A make or break moment: why $79,200 could act as a launchpad or a ceiling for bitcoin
items.desc: True Market Mean and Short-Term Holder cost basis form a critical $78.2K to $79.2K range that could define the next major move.

items.title: A $575 bet on a Shiba-themed token became $1.17 million in 5 days
items.desc: An anonymous wallet bought 2.79 billion ASTEROID tokens for $575 on April 17 and exited Tuesday for 503 ETH, worth $1.17 million, in one of the year's cleanest memecoin trades.

items.desc: Volo Protocol lost about $3.5 million from three vaults holding WBTC, XAUm, and USDC.

items.title: Russia passes crypto bill in first reading; permits use in foreign trade settlements
items.desc: The bill would prohibit the use of crypto for domestic payments but allow its use in foreign economic activities, according to TASS.

items.title: Bitcoin's 'Coinbase premium' just posted its longest bullish streak since October's record high of $126,000
items.desc: Bitcoin's Coinbase premium has been positive for 14 straight days, the longest bullish streak since BTC hit its all time high of $126,000 in October.

items.title: Privacy protocol Umbra shuts front end to stifle Kelp exploiters
items.desc: Umbra has shut down its front end to stop hackers, but says it can’t stop the use of its smart contracts or another version of its open-source front end.

items.title: Chinese crypto mogul Li Lin’s private trading arm is set to move into a Hong Kong-listed wealth firm
items.desc: The transfer effectively moves part of Li’s private trading operation into a public company where he is the largest shareholder.

items.title: Coinbase highlights Algorand, Aptos' work to mitigate quantum threat
items.desc: Coinbase’s quantum advisory board says quantum computing isn’t yet a threat, but has urged for upgrade work to begin, with some blockchains being less prepared than others.

items.title: Justin Sun sues World Liberty Financial over WLFI token freeze, governance exclusion
items.desc: Sun said the WLFI team refused to unfreeze his tokens, leaving him with no choice but to turn to the courts.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
items.desc: Bitcoin Core version 31.0 is now available for download. See the release notes for more information about the bug fixes in this release. If you have any questions, please stop by the #bitcoin IRC chatroom (IRC, web) and we’ll do our best to help you.

items.desc: How Ripple is testing RLUSD in Singapore’s MAS sandbox to enable conditional trade settlements, and why this does not amount to full regulatory approval.

items.title: Sui-based Volo Protocol exploited; team pledges to absorb losses
items.desc: Sui-based liquid staking platform Volo reported that it was exploited for roughly $3.5 million from its WBTC, XAUm, and USDC vaults.

items.desc: The 24-hour swap volume on THORChain surged to $394 million, significantly exceeding usual daily volumes of under $35 million.

items.title: US admiral calls Bitcoin an instrument for US ‘power projection’
items.desc: US Navy Admiral Samuel Paparo said Bitcoin’s proof-of-work technology has "really important" computer science applications when it comes to cybersecurity.

items.desc: Mark “Murch” Erhardt, Gustavo Flores Echaiz, and Mike Schmidt are joined by Remix7531 and Luis Schwab to discuss Newsletter #401.

items.desc: How did Michael Saylor's firm amass a record stash of Bitcoin? Here's a look back at how Strategy made such massive gains.

items.desc: Tempo announced the integration of ”stablecoin-powered payment infrastructure” with DoorDash, Stripe, Paradigm, Coastal Bank and ARQ.

items.title: Blockchain.com adds perpetual futures trading to self-custody wallets
items.desc: With a CFTC greenlight expected soon, industry momentum is building for the derivative contracts still only available to non-US investors.

items.title: Tether Asserts Stablecoin Dominance Over Circle's USDC Amid Major Crypto Hacks
items.desc: Tether's USDT market cap hit an all-time high, with DeFi users appearing to favor the stablecoin over USDC as a refuge from industry fallout.

items.title: New York Sues Coinbase and Gemini Over Prediction Markets, Seeking Billions in Damages
items.desc: "Gemini and Coinbase’s so-called prediction markets are just illegal gambling operations," alleged New York AG Letitia James.

items.title: Dogecoin Foundation, MoonPay Team to Donate 1 Million DOGE to Dog Charity
items.desc: Crypto payments firm MoonPay enabled contributions to dog welfare programs, marking a practical use case for Dogecoin.

items.title: New York Attorney General Sues Coinbase, Gemini Over Unlicensed Prediction Markets
items.desc: New York AG Letitia James filed suit against Coinbase and Gemini, alleging their prediction market platforms constitute illegal gambling operations lacking state licenses.

items.title: Aave Partially Unfreezes WETH After Kelp Bridge Exploit
items.desc: After attackers deposited rsETH from an exploited Kelp bridge and borrowed Wrapped ETH, Aave had frozen WETH across multiple markets.

items.title: Bitcoin Whales Accumulate 45,000 BTC as Warsh and Paparo Back Bitcoin’s Role
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoin Whales Accumulate 45,000 BTC as Warsh and Paparo Back Bitcoin’s Role Bitcoin rose to around $76,000 as whales accumulated 45,000 BTC and institutional inflows surged, while U.S. officials including Kevin Warsh and Admiral Paparo signaled growing acceptance of Bitcoin in finance and national security. This post Bitcoin Whales Accumulate 45,000 BTC as Warsh and Paparo Back Bitcoin’s Role first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: DoorDash Teams Up with Tempo on Stablecoin Payments for Its Global Marktplace

items.title: DoorDash to Pay Delivery Workers in Stablecoins via Stripe's Tempo Blockchain
items.desc: Delivery firm DoorDash will use Stripe's payments-focused blockchain, Tempo, to pay its Dashers in stablecoins in more than 40 countries.

items.desc: Ramp expands stablecoin access by launching USDT across Solana, Plasma, and Ethereum with seamless 1:1 USD conversion for global money movement.

items.desc: Since the freeze, ZachXBT reported that the attackers had begun moving funds from Ethereum mainnet to Bitcoin.

items.title: Privacy Boost, Sunnyside's Privacy SDK, Goes Live on Optimism Mainnet: Optimism
items.desc: Optimism's first privacy offering by core developer Sunnyside uses ZK and TEE hybrid technology to enable confidential computing for enterprises on the OP Stack.

items.title: DeFi Protocols Launch Joint Escape Hatch for Aave ETH Lenders and Loopers
items.desc: Fluid's aWETH Redemption Protocol, launched with Lido, Ether.fi, 1inch, 0x and Kyber, has processed $136M out of Aave's frozen WETH pool in 48 hours.

items.title: Aave Models $124M to $230M in Bad Debt From Kelp Exploit
items.desc: In a detailed incident report, Aave service providers quantified the protocol's exposure for the first time and outlined two scenarios depending on how Kelp DAO allocates the loss. LayerZero and Kelp continue to blame each other for the compromised bridge configuration.
